Review my implementation of US-028 for the Marketify project (repo at /Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify, branch `ralph/marketify-mvp`).

## Story
US-028: "Primitive: BottomSheet + Toast"

## Acceptance Criteria
- `src/components/primitives/BottomSheet.tsx` — gesture-driven dismissal, snap points, scrim
- `src/components/primitives/Toast.tsx` — global toast provider in app root, success/error/info variants, auto-dismiss with progress bar
- Mobile-mcp verification: open sheet, dismiss via drag, fire toast (done)
- Commit

## Files I changed (review these only)
- `src/components/primitives/BottomSheet.tsx` (new)
- `src/components/primitives/Toast.tsx` (new)
- `app/_layout.tsx` (wired ToastProvider between GestureHandlerRootView and Slot)
- `app/index.tsx` (extended preview screen to exercise both)

## Authoritative spec sections to check against
- `docs/design.md` §1.2 (palette incl semantic colors), §1.3 (typography), §1.4 (motion presets — SPRING_SNAPPY for modal slide-up), §1.5 (corners/borders/shadow), §3.3 (filters = bottom sheet 80% height), §6 inventory (lines 733–735 — Toast props `message, variant, duration`; BottomSheet props `children, snapPoints`), §7 anti-patterns (no modal-over-modal, hard-shadow only, spring timing), §8 pre-delivery checklist (allowFontScaling for UI chrome, useReducedMotion respected)
- `scripts/ralph/progress.txt` Codebase Patterns (top of file) — especially:
  - Reanimated `withReducedMotion<WithSpringConfig>(SPRING_SNAPPY, reduced)` generic pattern
  - `useSharedValue` not React state for layout-derived animation inputs (SkeletonCard pattern)
  - Shadow animation workaround (shadowOffset is not animatable — fade shadowOpacity + translate)
  - allowFontScaling={false} for micro labels
  - Non-interactive Pressables should set accessible={false}
  - Neubrutalist hard shadow is universal for standalone surfaces
- Existing primitives for convention match: `src/components/primitives/Button.tsx`, `Chip.tsx`, `SkeletonCard.tsx`

## What I want you to check
1. **Correctness bugs** — specifically in BottomSheet: any race where the sheet's translateY shared value initial value gets out of sync with `visible` prop on first render; any gesture-handler worklet closure captures that would misbehave when props change; any Modal+GestureHandlerRootView nesting issue.
2. **Toast ToastProvider** — timer cleanup, race between `show()` replacing a prior toast mid-dismiss, memory leak on unmount, hook throw behavior.
3. **Design-spec deviations** — motion preset, shadows (BottomSheet uses hard shadow pointing UP — is that spec-kosher?), colors, radii, typography.
4. **Accessibility** — Modal focus management, live-region on toast, dismiss targets, screen reader flow.
5. **Reduced-motion compliance** — both primitives should short-circuit or no-op animations when `useReducedMotion()` returns true.
6. **Convention drift** — do these files match the style/pattern of Button.tsx and Chip.tsx (import order, comment style, props shape, StyleSheet at bottom)?

Report only issues that are medium-priority or higher. For each: location (file:line), severity, what's wrong, and the recommended fix. Do NOT summarize what the code does — assume I've read it.

1	# Ralph Progress Log — Marketify
2	Started: (will be replaced on first run)
3	---
4	
5	## Codebase Patterns
6	*Append-only consolidated learnings. Each future iteration reads this BEFORE doing anything.*
7	
8	- **Authoritative specs are `docs/product-plan.md`, `docs/design.md`, `docs/tech-architecture.md`.** When the story is ambiguous, read the relevant section there. Do not invent product behavior.
9	- **Use Supabase MCP, not the supabase CLI**, for migrations, queries, edge functions, and type generation. Migrations are append-only and named `us_NNN_description`.
10	- **Use mobile-mcp for any UI verification.** Never claim a UI story is done without a screenshot from `mobile_take_screenshot`.
11	- **Use the `feature-dev:code-reviewer` subagent before every commit.** It catches drift across iterations that you can't see in your fresh context.
12	- **Delegate exploration.** If a story would have you read >3 files, spawn the `Explore` subagent first and work from its summary.
13	- **Always regenerate `src/types/supabase.ts`** after any schema migration. Commit the regenerated file in the same commit as the migration.
14	- **Use `bun`, not npm/yarn/pnpm.** It is what's installed locally.
15	- **JWT secret is `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET`**, separate from Supabase's built-in JWT secret. We sign our own per docs/product-plan.md §2.
16	- **Apify key is `APIFY_KEY`** in `.env`. Read via `process.env.APIFY_KEY` or `Deno.env.get("APIFY_KEY")`. Never paste into source.
17	- **Edge functions live in `supabase/functions/<name>/index.ts`** with shared utils under `supabase/functions/_shared/`.
18	- **Component organization:** primitives in `src/components/primitives/`, screen-specific in `src/screens/<screen>/`, shared composites in `src/components/shared/`.
19	- **No emoji in product UI** — Lucide icons only (per docs/design.md).
20	- **One role per account in v1** — no dual-role logic.
21	- **Run `get_advisors({type:'security'})` after every RLS or schema change.** Address every error-level finding before commit.
22	- **`expo lint` default glob is `['src', 'app', 'components']`.** Empty subdirs with only `.gitkeep` cause eslint to exit 2 ("all files ignored"). Keep at least one lintable `.ts`/`.tsx` file in any of those roots (`src/index.ts` with `export {};` was used for US-001).
23	- **Expo bootstrap workaround:** `create-expo-app .` refuses to run when the target dir has dotfiles/dirs it considers hostile. Bootstrap into a sibling folder (e.g. `.expo-init`), then move the generated files up. Preserves `.claude/`, `.env`, `docs/`, `scripts/`.
24	- **Reanimated 4.x babel plugin is `react-native-worklets/plugin`** (not the legacy `react-native-reanimated/plugin`). It lives in the `react-native-worklets` package — pin it explicitly via `bunx expo install react-native-worklets` so `bun install --production` won't drop it. Included by default in fresh Expo SDK 54 templates.
25	- **Prefer `bunx expo install <pkg>`** over `bun add` for native modules — it pins the SDK-compatible version. Expo's install runs `bun add` under the hood when a `bun.lock` is present.
26	- **App entry is `"main": "expo-router/entry"`** in package.json. The old `index.ts` + `App.tsx` from US-001 were removed. Root layout lives at `app/_layout.tsx`; initial route at `app/index.tsx`.
27	- **Expo Go + SDK 54 + new-arch + `<Stack>` from expo-router throws at runtime**: `Exception in HostFunction: TypeError: expected dynamic type 'boolean', but had type 'string'`. For US-002 the root uses `<Slot />` as the minimal root. US-031 will revisit with a dev-client build (`expo run:ios`) where Stack + Tabs should work.
28	- **iOS simulator boot flow for mobile-mcp verification:** `xcrun simctl boot <name>` → `open -a Simulator` → `bunx expo start --ios` in background → `xcrun simctl openurl <udid> exp://127.0.0.1:8081` if Expo Go doesn't auto-open the project. mobile-mcp's `mobile_open_url` blocks `exp://` schemes by default, so shell out.
29	- **Supabase project:** `marketify` (ref=`[REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF]`, region=`ap-southeast-1`). All MCP DB calls use this `project_id`. Pre-installed extensions: pgcrypto, uuid-ossp, pg_stat_statements, supabase_vault, pg_graphql. pg_cron + citext available on request.
30	- **Install extensions `with schema extensions`** (e.g. `create extension if not exists citext with schema extensions`) to keep advisors clean. `pg_cron` is the exception: Supabase requires it in `pg_catalog`, leave it unqualified.
31	- **Enum canonical list is docs/tech-architecture.md §4.2** (lines 399-427). After generating types, verify `src/types/supabase.ts` Constants block matches it exactly.
32	- **Local migration files live under `supabase/migrations/` with `YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_us_NNN_description.sql`.** Keep filename and MCP migration `name` (no timestamp prefix) consistent. Commit every applied migration — `list_migrations` is the DB side, the file is the source of truth for fresh applies.
33	- **`app_private` schema was created in US-003** via a companion migration (`us_003_app_private_schema`). Revoke is already in place for `anon` and `authenticated`. Triggers / security-definer functions in later stories should target this schema.
34	- **Reference `citext` as `extensions.citext` in every migration.** US-003 installed it with `with schema extensions`; the default `search_path` used by migrations does not include that schema, so an unqualified `citext` fails to resolve.
35	- **Supabase auto-enables RLS on new `public.*` tables** on this project. New table migrations will show up in `get_advisors({type:'security'})` as INFO-level `rls_enabled_no_policy` (not `rls_disabled`). These INFO findings are benign until policies land in US-009 — no commit-blocking action needed.
36	- **`listing_conditions` is a single data-driven table**, not separate pre/post tables. Discriminated by `condition_kind` enum ('pre' | 'post') per docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7 block 8. Pre-condition rows use metrics like `min_followers`; post-condition rows use `post_*` metrics. The story US-005 text named separate tables but the spec wins.
37	- **Circular FK pattern:** `listings.current_version_id` references `listing_versions(id)` but both tables are in the same migration. Create the `listings` table with the column nullable, create `listing_versions`, then `alter table public.listings add constraint ... foreign key ...`. Spec follows this pattern (§4.7 lines 659-661).
38	- **Supabase defaults views to `security_invoker=off` (effectively DEFINER)**; it will fail the `security_definer_view` advisor. Every view we create must be followed with `alter view <name> set (security_invoker = on)` to match the project standard at §4.1 line 375. Applied to `submission_reuse_view` in US-006.
39	- **RLS helper pattern (use for every policy that scopes to the caller):** two SQL `stable` helpers `public.current_user_id()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'sub', '')::uuid`) and `public.current_user_role()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'role', '')::public.user_role`). Both use `set search_path = ''` and schema-qualify every ref. `nullif` on the empty string makes a missing claim safely return NULL (all `id = current_user_id()` checks fail closed); for `current_user_role()` this is a hardening deviation from the spec's verbatim `(auth.jwt() ->> 'role')::user_role` — intentional, keeps the fn from raising on malformed JWTs. Policies call these helpers instead of inlining `auth.jwt()` for readability + single-source-of-truth. Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260417140000_us_009_rls_policies.sql`.
40	- **The `submission_reuse_view` → `submission_reuse_count(uuid)` function swap (US-009 resolution):** the view with `security_invoker=on` would be filtered by the caller's `submissions_lister_read` policy and hide cross-lister reuses (the ReuseBadge's entire purpose). The fix: **drop the view** and replace with a SECURITY DEFINER function `public.submission_reuse_count(p_submission_id uuid) returns integer` that (1) does its own ownership check via join through `submissions → applications → listings.lister_id = current_user_id()`, (2) returns NULL when the caller isn't the listing owner, (3) bypasses RLS to count cross-lister reuses. Revoked from public, granted to authenticated + service_role. Keep this pattern for any other cross-tenant aggregate the UI needs.
41	- **Verify RLS via a transactional `begin; ... rollback;` block:** seed fresh fixtures with known `auth.uid`s, then `set local role authenticated` + `set local "request.jwt.claims" = '{"sub":"<uuid>","role":"creator"}'` and assert row visibility via `insert into _asserts ...` rows that you `select *` at the end before rolling back. Important: `grant insert, select on _asserts to authenticated` before switching roles, else you'll get `permission denied for table _asserts`. `PERFORM` is PL/pgSQL only — don't use it outside a DO block. Check `information_schema.columns` before writing fixtures so INSERTs match real schema (columns migrate between blocks — e.g. `listing_version_id` lives on `applications`, not `submissions`).
42	- **Trigger function hardening pattern (mandatory for every new trigger / PL/pgSQL function in this repo):**
43	  1. Declare `set search_path = ''` on the function (CREATE OR REPLACE drops prior SET clauses, so restate it on every redefinition).
44	  2. With empty `search_path`, qualify EVERY user-defined ref: tables `public.<tbl>`, types/enum casts `public.<type>` (e.g. `'tiktok'::public.platform`), `app_private.<fn>` for sibling functions.
45	  3. For pg_catalog **functions** (`now()`, `hashtextextended()`, `pg_advisory_xact_lock()`, etc.): bare is fine — `pg_catalog` is always implicitly searched even with empty `search_path`. Schema-qualifying as `pg_catalog.<fn>()` also works for true functions.
46	  4. **GREATEST / LEAST / COALESCE / NULLIF are SQL parser constructs, NOT functions.** They CANNOT be schema-qualified (`pg_catalog.greatest()` raises 42883). Always write them bare.
47	  5. SECURITY DEFINER functions especially require empty `search_path` — without it, a caller-controlled search_path can shadow `public.<table>` and divert writes. The advisor `function_search_path_mutable` flags this at WARN.
48	- **US-008 TODO — out-of-order Apify webhook delivery:** `app_private.denorm_metrics` uses `greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at)` to guard the timestamp column, but the surrounding column UPDATEs (e.g. `tiktok_follower_count`) are unconditional. If a webhook with an older `fetched_at` lands after a newer one (different `apify_run_id`, so the dedup index doesn't catch it), the trigger will overwrite fresh denorm columns with stale values even though the timestamp itself refuses to roll back. The spec §15 has the same gap. Resolution when US-008 (apify-core / webhook receiver) lands: either add `where new.fetched_at >= public.creator_profiles.metrics_fetched_at` to each branch's UPDATE, or guard the whole denorm block on a max(fetched_at) check inside the advisory lock. Update the spec section in the same PR.
49	- **US-008 nice-to-have — advisory lock collision risk:** `app_private.denorm_metrics` uses single-arg `pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtextextended(...))`. Two distinct (social_link_id, scrape_mode) strings can collide to the same 64-bit key, causing unrelated creators to serialize unnecessarily. Not a correctness issue (only throughput). Standard fix is the two-arg form `pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext(social_link_id::text), hashtext(scrape_mode::text))`. Spec §15 codifies the single-arg form; address as a spec amendment when load matters.
50	- **`public.events` append-only is a social contract, not a DB-level guarantee.** US-008 reproduces the spec literally (`revoke all on public.events from anon, authenticated`), but `service_role` and `postgres` retain every grant including UPDATE/DELETE — by design per spec §4.4 lines 1083–1085 ("Service role only. No application read/write policy"). Any edge function or trigger that writes to `events` must treat INSERT as the only safe verb; there is no DB trigger that blocks UPDATE/DELETE. If stronger enforcement is ever desired, add a BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE trigger raising `RAISE EXCEPTION` (this would also block maintenance scripts — considered too restrictive for v1).
51	- **In PL/pgSQL with `set search_path = ''`, `text[] || 'literal'` resolves to `anyarray || anyarray` and crashes with `22P02 malformed array literal`.** Postgres chooses the array+array overload for unknown-type literals. Always cast the right-hand side: `changed_fields || 'price_cents'::text`. Typed text columns (e.g. `new.version_bump_reason`) work bare because the operator resolves to `anyarray || anyelement` when the right side has a concrete element type. This gotcha is independent of the `public.<type>` schema-qualification pattern already in Codebase Patterns. Resolved in US-010 via `us_010_fix_array_append` follow-up migration.
52	- **US-010 hard dependency for US-038 and US-049-055:** the cascade trigger (`app_private.bump_listing_version`) swaps `listings.current_version_id` to the new version, but it does NOT refresh the cached `listings.min_followers_tiktok` / `min_followers_instagram` threshold columns. Those columns are populated by §15b (`app_private.refresh_listing_thresholds` on `listing_conditions` + `app_private.refresh_thresholds_on_version_bump` on `listings.current_version_id`) which was intentionally scoped OUT of US-010 per its AC. Before any edge function that calls `app_private.request_listing_version_bump` ships, or the discover feed's eligibility filter (US-038) is wired up, §15b triggers MUST land — otherwise threshold columns silently go stale on every conditions-only bump, the feed's threshold index returns wrong eligibility, and the filter lies. Which story claims §15b is open (earliest candidate US-038; the campaign-creation wizard US-049-055 is the latest safe slot).
53	- **`pg_net.net.http_post(url := NULL, ...)` is a silent no-op, not an error.** When the URL or any required string is NULL (e.g. a `vault.decrypted_secrets` lookup misses), pg_net returns a request id without raising and `cron.job_run_details` logs the run as `succeeded`. Always wrap pg_net calls inside cron bodies in a PL/pgSQL `do $body$ ... $body$` block that resolves required arguments first and `RAISE EXCEPTION` when any are NULL — that surfaces the misconfiguration in `cron.job_run_details` instead of pretending the job ran. Pattern documented in `supabase/migrations/20260417163100_us_011_fix_silent_no_secret.sql`.
54	- **Vault-secret pattern for cron-invoked edge functions:** secrets live in `vault.decrypted_secrets` (named `marketify_<purpose>` so they cluster in the dashboard). Migrations DO NOT create the secrets — the actual key material is set out-of-band via the Supabase dashboard. Migrations document the required secret names in the header comment so the next operator knows what to populate. Pre-population, the cron raises and the failure is visible in `cron.job_run_details`. Required secrets so far: `marketify_project_url`, `marketify_service_role_key` (both for `cron-refresh-metrics`).
55	- **`pg_cron` job-name idempotency requires a guarded `cron.unschedule`.** `cron.schedule` raises on duplicate jobname; `cron.unschedule(text)` raises when the name is unknown. Always wrap in `do $$ begin if exists (select 1 from cron.job where jobname = '<name>') then perform cron.unschedule('<name>'); end if; end $$;` before re-scheduling. There is no upsert in the pg_cron 1.6 API.
56	- **Spec gap (§16 jobs not yet scheduled):** US-011 only schedules `mark-metrics-stale` and `cron-refresh-metrics` (per its AC). §16 also defines `fail-stuck-refreshing` (operationally important — backstops lost Apify webhooks, referenced by §5.2's overlap-guard guarantee), `prune-read-notifications`, `prune-signup-attempts`, `prune-expired-revocations`. None has an assigned story. `fail-stuck-refreshing` should land alongside the webhook receiver story (US-016+); the prune jobs can land with their respective table stories (`signup_attempts` + `revoked_sessions` tables don't exist yet — they belong with the JWT/session story group).
57	- **Spec gap (§16 staleness marker leaves `is_latest=false, status='fresh'` rows untouched):** the spec's UPDATE filters on `is_latest`, so any snapshot that was demoted by `denorm_metrics` (which sets `is_latest=false` but does NOT change `status`) and is also old will accumulate forever as `fresh`-but-orphaned. UI is unaffected (it reads `is_latest=true`), but storage hygiene degrades. Resolution: either drop the `is_latest` filter from the cron, or have `denorm_metrics` also set the demoted row's `status` to `stale`. Spec amendment, not a story-blocking fix.
58	- **Edge functions are Deno, not RN — exclude `supabase/` from the Expo toolchain.** tsc (`tsconfig.json` exclude) and eslint (`eslint.config.js` ignores) both skip `supabase/**`. Edge-function code imports `Deno.env`, uses Web Crypto, and runs under Supabase Edge Runtime. Caveat: tsc `exclude` does not prevent files pulled in via imports, so app code must never import from `supabase/functions/*`. Any cross-boundary types should be re-declared or lifted to `src/types/`.
59	- **JWT helper contract (`supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts`):** `signJwt({sub, role, session_id}, {ttlSeconds?})` + `verifyJwt(token): JwtClaims`. HS256 via Web Crypto. Secret from `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` (NOT the Supabase project JWT secret). Claims are `{sub, role, session_id, iat, exp}` — `session_id` is spec-required per §4.4 (story AC said only 4 claims; spec wins). `verifyJwt` enforces `alg=HS256`, exp > now, iat ≤ now+60s, and validates claim shapes. Any JSON/base64/atob malformation surfaces as `"Malformed JWT"`. Uses `crypto.subtle.verify` (not re-sign + compare) with a verify-only-usage key.
60	- **CORS headers shared module (`supabase/functions/_shared/cors.ts`):** `corsHeaders` allows `*` origin — safe because edge functions authenticate via `Authorization: Bearer <marketify_jwt>`, not cookies. Every edge function must splat this into responses and handle an `OPTIONS` pre-flight with `{ status: 204, headers: corsHeaders }`.
61	- **Atomic multi-insert edge-function flow uses a PL/pgSQL RPC, not back-to-back supabase-js inserts.** Two `supabase.rpc`/`.insert()` calls are two separate PostgREST transactions, so a mid-flight crash leaves orphan rows. The established pattern (US-014) is a single `public.<flow>_rpc` PL/pgSQL function invoked via `supabase.rpc(...)` from the edge function — PL/pgSQL wraps the body in an implicit transaction. Catch `unique_violation` with `GET STACKED DIAGNOSTICS v_constraint = constraint_name` and return `{error: CODE}` jsonb; let everything else `RAISE` so the edge function can log+500. Implicit-constraint names (`<table>_<column>_key`) are Postgres defaults — ALWAYS comment the expected names in the exception block, since a future migration that renames them would silently downgrade 409s to 500s. Revoke execute from anon/authenticated/public; grant only to service_role (no SECURITY DEFINER needed — service_role already has BYPASSRLS).
62	- **Supabase edge-function secrets are set via `supabase secrets set --env-file <tmpfile> --project-ref <ref>`, not via Supabase MCP.** The MCP has no secrets tool. Create a temp file containing `NAME=value`, run the CLI, delete the temp file. Never echo the secret value into chat. After setting, re-invoke the edge function — secret names are available via `Deno.env.get(...)`. Running the edge function before the secret is set surfaces as a 500 at the point the helper tries to read it (for `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET`, that's `signJwt` → `"MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET is not set"` → caught → returned as `{error: "JWT_ERROR"}` in the current auth-* pattern).
63	- **Edge-function smoke tests live under `scripts/test/<fn-name>.ts`, invoked with `bun run`.** Read `SUPABASE_URL` + `SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` from `process.env` (bun auto-loads `.env`). Send `Authorization: Bearer <anon>` + `apikey: <anon>` headers even for `verify_jwt=false` functions — the Supabase gateway still requires a valid apikey at the edge. Use `Date.now()` for unique test fixture values so repeat runs don't collide. Every conflict branch (e.g. 409 USERNAME_TAKEN vs 409 EMAIL_TAKEN) gets its own case — the constraint-name mapping in the RPC's exception handler is only validated by cases that actually hit each branch. Clean up test rows via `execute_sql` after each run.
64	- **Anonymous `unique` declarations in CREATE TABLE get Postgres default names (`<table>_<column>_key`)** — e.g. `username citext not null unique` on `public.users` produces `users_username_key`. Any PL/pgSQL that inspects `constraint_name` depends on this. Verify via `select conname from pg_constraint where conrelid = 'public.<table>'::regclass` before hardcoding a name. If you later want to rename, drop+re-add with explicit `constraint <name> unique (col)` — and update every dependent exception handler in the same migration.
65	- **Apify REST endpoint + slug encoding:** use `POST https://api.apify.com/v2/acts/<rest-slug>/runs?token=<APIFY_KEY>&waitForFinish=<secs>`. REST slugs replace `/` with `~` (`clockworks~tiktok-scraper`, `apify~instagram-scraper`). `waitForFinish` makes Apify block up to N seconds before returning the run object — if the run exceeds N, Apify returns `status=RUNNING` and webhooks pick up the terminal state later. Response shape is `{ data: { id, defaultDatasetId, status, ... } }`. For this project the wrapper at `supabase/functions/_shared/apify.ts` uses direct REST (not the `apify-client` SDK) because Deno in Edge Runtime handles plain fetch cleanly and `Deno.env.get("APIFY_KEY")` is the sanctioned way to read the token.
66	- **Never echo external API error bodies into thrown Error strings when the request URL carries a secret in the query string.** Apify (and several other providers) sometimes include the request URL in error payloads, so `throw new Error(\`... ${body}\`)` can land the token in edge-function logs. Throw a fixed-string error with status + slug only; if you need the body for debugging, log it separately with redaction.
67	- **Deno tests for `_shared/` modules live alongside them as `<name>.test.ts` and run with `deno test --allow-env supabase/functions/_shared/<name>.test.ts`.** Assertions come from `jsr:@std/assert@1`. Mock fetch by replacing `globalThis.fetch` with a spy that records `{url, init}` and returns a canned `Response`; always restore in a `try/finally`. Deno isn't on every dev machine; `brew install deno` on macOS is a one-liner. If Deno isn't available in a given iteration, document it in the progress entry and have the CI for this run the tests.
68	- **Public async wrappers must be declared `async` if they do any synchronous validation that might throw**, so the throw surfaces as a Promise rejection rather than a synchronous exception. `assertRejects` in `@std/assert` fails with "Function throws when expected to reject" if the function throws synchronously — a real footgun that can only be caught by actually running the test.
69	- **Constant-time dual-secret compare for rotation-aware webhook auth:** edge functions that authenticate third-party webhooks via a shared secret must (a) compare in constant time (XOR-accumulate over `max(len(a), len(b))` bytes, iterate the longer one), (b) check BOTH `<SECRET>` and `<SECRET>_PREVIOUS` env vars without short-circuit, and (c) return false if neither env var is set. The second point is subtle: `return timingSafeEqual(a, current) || timingSafeEqual(a, previous)` short-circuits when the first matches, which means an attacker who has recovered `current` can time-distinguish whether `previous` is configured. Correct shape is `let matchA = current ? timingSafeEqual(provided, current) : false; let matchB = previous ? timingSafeEqual(provided, previous) : false; return matchA || matchB` — both evaluated unconditionally. Reference: `supabase/functions/apify-webhook/index.ts` `verifyWebhookSecret`.
70	- **Third-party-webhook-receiver edge functions deploy with `verify_jwt=false`** (Supabase MCP `deploy_edge_function({verify_jwt: false})`). The provider cannot send an `Authorization: Bearer <marketify-jwt>` — authentication is via the provider's own header (e.g. `X-Apify-Webhook-Secret`). 71	- **Webhook-receiver idempotency via service-role RPC + ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING:** the receiver's persistence step goes through a PL/pgSQL RPC that `INSERT ... Paired with a partial unique index on the run-id column, replays of the same webhook are safe. Revoke the RPC from anon/authenticated/public and grant only to service_role. The RPC is invoked by the edge function with a service-role Supabase client — NO `SECURITY DEFINER` needed because service_role already has BYPASSRLS. Reference: `public.apify_webhook_persist_tiktok_profile` + `metric_snapshots_run_uniq` (US-006/016).
72	- **Scope env-var reads to the branch that actually needs them.** A single top-of-handler `Deno.env.get("OPTIONAL_KEY")` guard that 500s on missing keys will trip branches that don't need the key. Move the lookup inside the conditional, and only return `SERVER_MISCONFIGURED` when you actually can't proceed.
73	- **`supabase/functions/<name>-live.ts` convention for Apify-touching e2e tests:** any smoke test that spends real Apify credits lives alongside the cheap-path smoke as `<fn>-live.ts`, is NOT part of the default test suite, and documents what it costs + what external state it touches. US-016 used `clockworks~tiktok-scraper` against `@tiktok` (cheap + stable + public) with seed `social_link_id = 11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111020` — good reference fixture for future Apify-touching stories. 74	- **Apify `payloadTemplate` requires `shouldInterpolateStrings: true` for quoted `"{{placeholder}}"` substitution.** Without this flag, quoted placeholders stay literal (`"{{resource.id}}"` delivered as-is) and the receiver's validation layer 400s. Bare unquoted `{{var}}` substitutes raw JSON but breaks `JSON.stringify(payloadTemplate)` because the placeholder isn't valid JSON. Rule: every webhook spec we build has `shouldInterpolateStrings: true` and uses quoted placeholders exclusively. Reference: `supabase/functions/_shared/apify.ts` `ApifyWebhookSpec` + `supabase/functions/auth-signup-creator/index.ts`.
75	- **Apify transitional run statuses are READY, RUNNING, TIMING-OUT, ABORTING.** When `waitForFinish` expires before a run reaches a terminal state, Apify returns one of these four — in all four cases the run continues and the registered webhook will still fire the terminal event later. Map all four to 'refreshing' in any caller that derives a metrics-status from the immediate actor-run response; only `SUCCEEDED` should map to 'fresh' and any other (FAILED/TIMED-OUT/ABORTED) to 'failed'. Call shape: `withSpring(target, withReducedMotion<WithSpringConfig>(SPRING_SOFT, reduced))` — the **explicit `<WithSpringConfig>` generic is mandatory** because the presets are declared `as const satisfies WithSpringConfig` which narrows them to readonly literal types; under `strict` TS those literals don't satisfy the helper's `C extends {reduceMotion?: ReduceMotion}` constraint without widening. Interactive lifted surfaces collapse the shadow on press via the animated `shadowOpacity` pattern; read-only lifted surfaces (StatusPill) just spread `shadows.hard` into their static style. 1	# Marketify — UI/UX Design Document
2	
3	*Mobile app for the creator marketing industry. Expo + React Native + Reanimated + Supabase.*
4	
5	---
6	
7	## 1. Design Language
8	
9	### 1.1 Chosen Style
10	
11	**Neubrutalism** as the primary language, with disciplined restraint for mobile comfort.
12	
13	**Why it fits Marketify:**
14	- The marketplace/directory pattern (Vibrant & Block-based) is the top match; Neubrutalism is its most purposeful expression — bold borders, hard offset shadows, flat pop colors.
15	- The pattern rewards scannability — hard black outlines on CampaignCards make bounties feel like physical "cards on a board."
16	- WCAG AAA by construction (high contrast), Excellent performance — critical for mid-tier Android.
17	- Differentiated. TikTok is dark-glassmorphic, Instagram is quiet gradient, every campaign-management tool (Grin, Aspire) is corporate-blue SaaS. Neubrutalism carves out a clear creator-native identity.
18	
19	**Disciplines to keep it mobile-friendly (not a desktop stunt):**
20	- Borders are 2px, not 4px.
21	- Shadows are 3px offset max, never 6–8px.
22	- Corner radius is 12px on cards, 999px on pills — not 0. Pure 0 feels brittle on touch.
23	- No intentionally "broken" layouts. Brutalism's anti-design aesthetic is skipped in favor of its craft elements only.
24	
25	### 1.2 Color Palette
26	
27	Base palette anchored on the **"Creator Economy Platform"** colors (pink + orange CTA), with semantic statuses added.
28	
29	| Role | Token | Hex | Usage |
30	|---|---|---|---|
31	| Primary | `--primary` | `#EC4899` | Brand, active tab icon, primary surfaces, logo mark |
32	| Primary Soft | `--primary-soft` | `#FDF2F8` | Primary-tinted backgrounds, selected chip bg |
33	| Primary Deep | `--primary-deep` | `#831843` | Primary text on soft bg, pressed states |
34	| Accent (CTA) | `--cta` | `#F97316` | All primary action buttons, "Apply", "Submit", "Create Campaign" |
35	| Accent Deep | `--cta-deep` | `#9A3412` | CTA pressed state |
36	| Ink (text) | `--ink` | `#0F172A` | All body and heading text, hard borders |
37	| Ink-70 | `--ink-70` | `#475569` | Secondary text, captions |
38	| Ink-40 | `--ink-40` | `#94A3B8` | Tertiary / disabled text |
39	| Surface | `--surface` | `#FFFFFF` | Card bodies |
40	| Canvas | `--canvas` | `#FFF9F2` | App background (warm off-white — matches orange CTA undertone) |
41	| Hairline | `--hairline` | `#E5E7EB` | Dividers inside cards |
42	| **Success (approved)** | `--success` | `#16A34A` | Approved submissions, approved applications, eligible checks |
43	| Success Soft | `--success-soft` | `#DCFCE7` | Approved pill bg |
44	| **Danger (rejected)** | `--danger` | `#DC2626` | Rejected submissions, rejected applications |
45	| Danger Soft | `--danger-soft` | `#FEE2E2` | Rejected pill bg, ineligibility warning |
46	| **Warning (pending)** | `--warning` | `#F59E0B` | Pending status, waiting-for-review states |
47	| Warning Soft | `--warning-soft` | `#FEF3C7` | Pending pill bg |
48	| **Cancelled** | `--cancelled` | `#6B7280` | Auto-cancelled applications |
49	| Cancelled Soft | `--cancelled-soft` | `#F3F4F6` | Cancelled pill bg |
50	| Shadow | `--shadow` | `#0F172A` | Neubrutalist hard shadow (always ink, never tinted) |
51	
52	**Contrast targets (measured against `--canvas` `#FFF9F2`):**
53	- Body text (`--ink` `#0F172A`): **17.8:1** — passes WCAG AAA.
54	- Secondary text (`--ink-70` `#475569`): **7.9:1** — passes AAA.
55	- Primary brand pink (`--primary` `#EC4899`) on canvas: **~3.3:1** — passes WCAG AA for large text (18pt+ or 14pt bold) and UI components, but **does not clear AAA**. Primary pink is therefore **never used for body text** — only for brand marks, large display accents, active-tab icons (paired with a text label), and soft-tinted backgrounds. Where pink needs to carry meaning on light backgrounds at body size, we use `--primary-deep` `#831843` (contrast ~11:1, AAA).
56	- CTA orange (`--cta` `#F97316`) is a background color under black label text (17.8:1), so the button itself is AAA; the orange is never used as foreground text.
57	
58	**Dark mode:** deferred for MVP. Neubrutalism translates cleanly (invert canvas → `#0B0B0F`, keep shadow as a white 1px border with a `rgba(0,0,0,0.4)` offset), but it doubles visual QA.
59	
60	### 1.3 Font Pairing
61	
62	**"Startup Bold"** — `Clash Display` for headings, `Satoshi` for body. Both via `expo-font` (Fontshare CDN or self-host). Fallbacks: `Outfit` for headings, `Rubik` for body.
63	
64	| Role | Font | Weight | Size (mobile) |
65	|---|---|---|---|
66	| Display (screen titles, campaign titles) | Clash Display | 600–700 | 28 / 22 |
67	| Heading | Clash Display | 600 | 20 |
68	| Subhead | Satoshi | 600 | 17 |
69	| Body | Satoshi | 500 | 15 |
70	| Caption | Satoshi | 500 | 13 |
71	| Micro (pills, chip labels) | Satoshi | 700 | 11 (uppercase, `letter-spacing: 0.08em`) |
72	| Monospace (URLs, handles @xxx) | JetBrains Mono | 500 | 14 |
73	
74	**Usage rules:**
75	- Display font is *only* for titles and hero metric numbers. Never stack two Clash Display elements in the same card.
76	- User-generated content (campaign descriptions) bumps to 16px.
77	- Line-height: 1.55 body, 1.15 display.
78	- Line-length: max 65 characters → content width caps at ~340px on a 390px screen.
79	
80	**Dynamic Type / system font scale.** Honor iOS Dynamic Type and Android font scale up to **130%** on all text — all sizes above scale proportionally via `allowFontScaling={true}` (RN default). **Cap at 130%** to protect Neubrutalism's fixed line-heights (1.15 display) and the 340px content-width cap, both of which break visually past that threshold (letters start touching; cards overflow gutters). Above 130% we clamp the scale factor in a top-level `Text.defaultProps.maxFontSizeMultiplier = 1.3` setting — the OS-level preference is still reflected up to the cap, just not beyond it. For users who need more, the v2 accessibility settings screen will expose a "Full size-up (may break layout)" escape hatch. Pills, status chips, and tab-bar labels use `allowFontScaling={false}` because their containers are fixed — they're UI chrome, not content.
81	
82	### 1.4 Motion Language (Reanimated)
83	
84	**Spring presets** (define in `animations/springs.ts`):
85	```
86	soft:    { damping: 20, stiffness: 180, mass: 1 }   // cards, chips
87	snappy:  { damping: 18, stiffness: 260, mass: 1 }   // tab switch, pill transitions
88	bouncy:  { damping: 12, stiffness: 220, mass: 1 }   // status change celebration
89	```
90	
91	**Timing presets:**
92	```
93	micro:  150ms  ease-out     // press feedback
94	short:  220ms  ease-out     // enter/exit
95	medium: 320ms  cubic-bezier(0.2, 0.8, 0.2, 1)  // screen transitions
96	```
97	
98	**Motion rules:**
99	1. **Screen transitions** — stack push uses native `slide_from_right` (expo-router default). Modals use `modal` presentation with a spring slide-up (snappy).
100	2. **Press feedback** — every pressable scales to 0.97 on `pressIn` (spring.soft), returns on `pressOut`. Use `Pressable` + `useAnimatedStyle` with `useSharedValue`. Never use `TouchableOpacity`.

100	2. **Press feedback** — every pressable scales to 0.97 on `pressIn` (spring.soft), returns on `pressOut`. Use `Pressable` + `useAnimatedStyle` with `useSharedValue`. Never use `TouchableOpacity`.
101	3. **Status change (pending → approved/rejected)** — StatusPill morphs color with a 320ms color interpolation + one-shot bouncy scale (1 → 1.12 → 1). Approved gets confetti-lite: 8 small squares in primary/cta/success burst upward from the pill, fade over 800ms. Rejected gets a gentle left-right shake (3 cycles, ±4px, 120ms each). Both respect `prefers-reduced-motion`. **Deep-link arrivals** (user tapped the push notification and the Submission Detail screen is the first screen they land on) — the celebration anchor is the StatusPill inside the just-mounted screen, triggered 220ms after mount (after the screen-slide settles) so the burst reads as a response to the arrival, not a hanging-over-from-last-screen artifact. If the screen mounts with `status` already `approved` (i.e. the user arrived via deep link, not via an in-app realtime update), we still fire the celebration exactly once, gated by a `celebration_seen_for_submission_id` entry in MMKV so revisits don't re-trigger.
102	4. **Pull-to-refresh on metrics** — custom refresh control. Vertical "pull" reveals a Clash Display counter that rolls up (`withTiming` + numerical interpolation). On success, new value "slams" into place with bouncy spring and `✓` fades in. Delta shows `+1.2K` in success green for 1.5s.
103	5. **Card interactions (CampaignCard)** — on press, the card drops its hard shadow (translate `3px,3px` → `0,0`) and moves `translateX/Y(2px,2px)` to simulate a physical press. Spring.soft.
104	6. **Eligibility check animation** — tap "Apply" → criteria animate in a staggered list (each row fades + slides 8px from left, 60ms stagger). Passing conditions show a green check drawing in (SVG stroke-dashoffset, 280ms). Failing conditions shake once and fade to muted.
105	7. **Tab bar** — active tab icon morphs from `strokeWidth=1.5` outline to `strokeWidth=2.5` + filled enclosed areas via a paired ship-in-repo SVG (crossfaded, 180ms). Active indicator is a 4px bar under the icon (spring.snappy).
106	8. **FlatList entries** — on first mount, cards stagger in 40ms apart (translateY 16 → 0, opacity 0 → 1). Skip for subsequent renders. **Skip on screen re-entry within the same session** — a `useFocusEffect`-tracked `hasMountedOnce` ref gates the stagger so re-entering Feed from a pushed detail screen (or switching tabs back) doesn't replay the animation. Pull-to-refresh does *not* re-stagger either; new rows fade in individually (`LinearTransition` via `react-native-reanimated`'s layout animation API, 180ms). Full app relaunches reset the ref — the stagger is a first-impression-per-session flourish, not a per-mount one.
107	
108	**Performance:** all worklets, `runOnUI` for imperative runs. No `Animated` API. Gestures via `react-native-gesture-handler` + Reanimated v3.
109	
110	**Reduced motion:** wrap spring helpers in `useReducedMotion()`. Replace springs with `withTiming(value, { duration: 0 })` or short 120ms fade. Confetti and shake become no-ops; color transitions remain.
111	
112	### 1.5 Component Personality
113	
114	| Attribute | Decision |
115	|---|---|
116	| Corners | Cards 12px, buttons 10px, pills 999px, inputs 10px, images 8px. |
117	| Borders | 2px solid `--ink` on every card, button, input, chip. |
118	| Shadow | `offset (3, 3), color: --ink, opacity: 1, radius: 0` — signature hard shadow. Pressed collapses to (0,0). |
119	| Elevation | Two tiers: "flat" (no shadow) and "lifted" (hard shadow). No z-stacking beyond modal overlay. |
120	| Iconography | **Lucide React Native** exclusively. 24×24 viewBox, 2px stroke. Tab bar uses **stroke-weight toggle**, not filled variants — Lucide doesn't ship filled glyphs for every icon we need (`video`, `megaphone`, `inbox`). Inactive tab: `strokeWidth={1.5}`; active tab: `strokeWidth={2.5}` + fills the glyph's enclosed areas (e.g. compass needle) via a paired `fill` SVG we ship ourselves for the 4 creator + 4 lister tab icons. No emoji. |
121	| Imagery | Campaign sample videos get 2px ink border + hard shadow. Thumbnail aspect 9:16 for TikTok/Reels, 1:1 for avatars. |
122	| Empty-state illustrations | Geometric line drawings using primary/cta/ink only. No stock illustration libraries. |
123	| Haptics | `expo-haptics` — Light on press, Medium on status change, Success/Error on approve/reject arrivals. |

140	
141	*(Note: creator onboarding is handle-based — no OAuth, no platform consent screens. Our Apify scrapers pull public profile data from the handle alone. Handles are unverified in v1; there is no "claim your account" flow. The app simply trusts the creator to enter their own handle.)*
142	
143	### 2.2 Creator Screens

173	| Profile | `/(lister)/profile` | Company profile | "Edit" |

176	### 2.4 Shared / System

184	### 2.5 Empty & Error States (inline)
185	
186	- Feed empty (no campaigns): "Nothing live right now." + "Notify me" toggle.
187	- Feed empty (no eligible): see §5.5.

194	**Age-bucketed nudges for non-expiring inboxes.** Applications and submissions do not auto-expire, so an inbox is never "full" in the OS sense — but stale rows rot quietly. On inboxes where any open row is older than a threshold, the list prepends a soft-warning banner (not an empty state — the list is non-empty):

Primary pink is therefore **never used for body text** — only for brand marks, large display accents, active-tab icons (paired with a text label), and soft-tinted backgrounds. Where pink needs to carry meaning on light backgrounds at body size, we use `--primary-deep` `#831843` (contrast ~11:1, AAA).

230	**Inbox model — relationship to §2.3 IA.** §2.3 lists Applications Inbox and Submissions Inbox as *campaign-scoped* routes (`/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/applications` and `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/submissions`) because review always happens in-context for a specific campaign. The **Inbox tab** (`/(lister)/inbox`) is a cross-campaign roll-up — same underlying data, different entry point:
231	
232	- **Segmented header:** `Applications · Submissions` (segmented control, not two tabs — keeps the 4-tab bar).
233	- **Default segment:** whichever has the higher unreviewed count; ties go to Submissions (higher creator-anxiety cost per day of delay).
234	- **Row grouping:** grouped by campaign with `SectionHeader` rows; tapping a row pushes into the campaign-scoped review screen (same route as §2.3).
235	- **Empty state:** if both segments empty, "Nothing to review. Good time to post another bounty." with a CTA button to Create Campaign.
236	
237	So the campaign-scoped routes are the sources of truth; the Inbox tab is a lens. This matches how lister work actually happens — sometimes you think "that Nike campaign has applicants" (campaign-first), sometimes "do I have anything to review?" (inbox-first).

248	| Submission Review (lister) | Stack push with video | Back-swipe preserved. |
249	| Submission Composer | Stack push | Mid-flow from application detail. |
250	| Confirmations | Alert-style modal | Short, high-stakes. |

259	| Creator app approved | `marketify://applications/:id` | Application Detail with animated status change | Applied tab → Application Detail |

---

Reviewing `src/components/primitives/BottomSheet.tsx`, `src/components/primitives/Toast.tsx`, `app/_layout.tsx`, and `app/index.tsx` on branch `ralph/marketify-mvp` against the story AC, design spec, codebase patterns, and existing primitive conventions.

---

## Critical Issues

**1. BottomSheet: `translateY` initial value races with `sheetHeight` on first render**

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/src/components/primitives/BottomSheet.tsx`, line 74

Confidence: 95

`translateY` is initialized with `useSharedValue(sheetHeight)` where `sheetHeight` is derived from `useMemo`. On the very first render the shared value is correctly set to `sheetHeight`. However, if `screenHeight` changes (rotation, split-screen, font-scale change that triggers a new `useWindowDimensions` read), `sheetHeight` changes via `useMemo`, but `translateY.value` is never reassigned to track the new `sheetHeight` — it keeps the stale pixel value from the previous render cycle. The `useEffect` that drives open/close only fires on `visible` or `sheetHeight` changes, which means if the sheet is currently open (visible=true) and `sheetHeight` recalculates, the effect re-fires and springs to `0` (correct). But if `visible` is false and `sheetHeight` grows (e.g., device rotated), `translateY.value` may now be less than the new `sheetHeight`, leaving the sheet partially visible before the effect's spring completes. More critically, the gesture's `gestureStart.value` captures the live `translateY.value` on pan start — after a rotation with a stale `translateY` the snap math references an offset that doesn't correspond to the actual rendered position.

Recommended fix: In the `useEffect`, always set `translateY.value = sheetHeight` synchronously before starting the close spring, and add a separate effect that resets `translateY.value = sheetHeight` immediately whenever `sheetHeight` changes and `!visible`:

```ts
useEffect(() => {
  if (!visible) {
    translateY.value = sheetHeight; // sync reset, no animation needed when closed
  }
}, [sheetHeight, visible, translateY]);
```

---

**2. BottomSheet: hard shadow points upward but `shadows.hard` token has `height: 3` (downward)**

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/src/components/primitives/BottomSheet.tsx`, lines 161, 196–197

Confidence: 90

The sheet's `styles.sheet` sets `shadowOffset: { width: 0, height: -3 }` in the StyleSheet, but then `shadows.hard` is also spread onto the same view (line 161: `[styles.sheet, { height: sheetHeight }, shadows.hard, sheetAnimatedStyle, style]`). `shadows.hard` defines `shadowOffset: { width: 3, height: 3 }`. Because `shadows.hard` comes after `styles.sheet` in the array, the `{ width: 3, height: 3 }` token **overrides** the intended `{ width: 0, height: -3 }` from `styles.sheet`. The sheet ends up with a downward-right shadow identical to every other surface — the upward shadow direction described in the comment is silently discarded. On iOS, `shadowOffset` is a plain object and later entries in the style array win.

Additionally, the spec (§1.5, §7 anti-patterns, and Codebase Patterns "Neubrutalist lifted tier is universal") mandates `shadows.hard` (offset 3,3) on all standalone surfaces — the upward variant is not sanctioned by the spec. The comment in `styles.sheet` says "shadow on a bottom-pinned sheet reads upward" but there is no explicit spec permission for this variant. If an upward shadow is intentional, it cannot be expressed via the shared token and must be a standalone style that does not also spread `shadows.hard`.

Recommended fix: Remove `shadowOffset` from `styles.sheet` and use `shadows.hard` unmodified (the spec standard), or — if the team decides upward shadow is correct — remove `shadows.hard` from the JSX spread and instead define all shadow props inline in `styles.sheet` without relying on the token.

---

**3. BottomSheet: `GestureHandlerRootView` nested inside `Modal` without `style={{ flex: 1 }}`**

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/src/components/primitives/BottomSheet.tsx`, line 150

Confidence: 85

`GestureHandlerRootView` receives `style={styles.root}` where `styles.root` has `flex: 1` and `justifyContent: 'flex-end'`. This is correct, but the containing `Modal` has no explicit size — Modals on Android with `transparent={true}` sometimes do not honor `flex: 1` on children if there is no wrapping view with an explicit size. The established Android workaround (required when nesting GHRE inside a Modal) is to ensure the GHRE wrapper also receives an explicit `style={{ flex: 1 }}`. The `styles.root` already has `flex: 1`, so this is satisfied here — but the style also sets `justifyContent: 'flex-end'` directly on the GHRE, which means touch events on the transparent upper region (the scrim tap area) are delivered to a `Pressable` sitting inside an `Animated.View` that itself is a sibling at `StyleSheet.absoluteFillObject`. This structure is fine on iOS. On Android with `react-native-gesture-handler` v2+, a `GestureDetector` that wraps only the bottom sheet view (not the backdrop) may fail to recognize gestures if the backdrop `Pressable` intercepts the touch first since both sit under the same GHRE root. This is a known footgun. The safest fix is to make the backdrop's `Pressable` a sibling of the GHRE entirely (placing it outside), or to ensure `pointerEvents="box-none"` is set on the backdrop's `Animated.View` so taps fall through to gesture handlers.

Recommended fix: Add `pointerEvents="box-none"` to the backdrop `Animated.View` (line 151) so the `GestureDetector`'s touch routing is not preempted on Android. The `Pressable` child can still receive the tap:

```tsx
<Animated.View pointerEvents="box-none" style={[styles.backdrop, backdropAnimatedStyle]}>
  <Pressable style={StyleSheet.absoluteFill} onPress={onDismiss} ... />
</Animated.View>
```

---

## Important Issues

**4. Toast: `clearTimer` is not stable — `useCallback` over a plain function causes stale-closure risk on unmount**

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/src/components/primitives/Toast.tsx`, lines 77–82, 106

Confidence: 88

`clearTimer` is defined as a bare `const` arrow function inside the component body (not wrapped in `useCallback`). It closes over `timerRef`, which is stable (a `useRef`). This is safe for the `show`/`hide` callbacks that call it inline. However, the unmount cleanup at line 106 is `useEffect(() => () => clearTimer(), [])`. Because `clearTimer` is recreated on every render but the effect captures the closure from the first render (empty deps), it will always call the `clearTimer` from render #1 — which closes over `timerRef.current` from render #1. Since `timerRef` itself is a stable ref object, `timerRef.current` is always the latest timer ID regardless of which closure is called, so this is not actually a bug in practice. However, the pattern is fragile: if `clearTimer` is ever refactored to reference any non-ref state, the empty-dep cleanup effect will silently break. The conventional fix is to declare `clearTimer` with `useCallback` (no deps since it only touches `timerRef`) or move it into the `useEffect`:

```ts
const clearTimer = useCallback(() => {
  if (timerRef.current) {
    clearTimeout(timerRef.current);
    timerRef.current = null;
  }
}, []);
```

This is low-risk now but worth fixing for resilience, especially given the progress.txt note about worklet/closure hygiene.

---

**5. Toast: `ToastSurface` `translateY` shared value is not reset synchronously before the enter spring**

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/src/components/primitives/Toast.tsx`, lines 137–141

Confidence: 92

When a new toast replaces a prior one mid-display, `ToastSurface` receives a new `toast.id`. The `useEffect` fires, sets `translateY.value = -140` synchronously and then immediately starts `withSpring(0, ...)`. This is the correct pattern (mirrors `SkeletonCard`). However, `ToastSurface` is only mounted when `toast !== null` (line 113). When a second `show()` fires while the first toast is still visible, `setToast` replaces the `InternalToast` object. React keeps the same `ToastSurface` instance (no unmount/remount because the conditional renders the same component type). The `cancelAnimation` in the cleanup of the previous effect run will cancel the prior spring — but only after React schedules the cleanup, which happens asynchronously after the new effect fires. The sequence is: new `toast.id` → new effect fires → `translateY.value = -140` (snap) → `withSpring(0)` starts → then the prior effect's cleanup runs `cancelAnimation(translateY)`. **The `cancelAnimation` from the cleanup cancels the newly-started spring**, leaving the toast frozen at -140 (off-screen). This is a genuine race that will reliably reproduce when `show()` is called while a toast is already displaying.

The fix is to cancel the prior animation inside the new effect body before setting the reset value:

```ts
useEffect(() => {
  cancelAnimation(translateY);
  cancelAnimation(progress);
  translateY.value = -140;
  translateY.value = withSpring(0, withReducedMotion<WithSpringConfig>(SPRING_SNAPPY, reduced));
  // ...
  return () => {
    cancelAnimation(translateY);
    cancelAnimation(progress);
  };
}, [toast.id, toast.duration, reduced, translateY, progress]);
```

---

**6. BottomSheet: `accessibilityRole` missing on the sheet surface itself; backdrop Pressable focus order is wrong**

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/src/components/primitives/BottomSheet.tsx`, lines 151–168

Confidence: 85

The sheet's `Animated.View` has `accessibilityViewIsModal={true}` and `accessibilityLabel`, which is correct and traps VoiceOver focus within the modal. However, the backdrop `Pressable` (line 152–157) is a sibling of the `GestureDetector`/sheet and sits earlier in DOM order. With `accessibilityViewIsModal` on the sheet, iOS VoiceOver should skip elements outside the modal — but `accessibilityViewIsModal` is set on the sheet `Animated.View`, not on the `GestureHandlerRootView` root. VoiceOver scans from the root outward; the backdrop `Pressable` is not a descendant of the `accessibilityViewIsModal` node, so it may still receive focus before the sheet content, making the first swipe target "Dismiss sheet" rather than the sheet's contents. The standard pattern is to put `accessibilityViewIsModal` on the outermost container (here the `GestureHandlerRootView`) or to give the backdrop `accessible={false}` and expose only the sheet's children.

Recommended fix: Move `accessibilityViewIsModal` to the `GestureHandlerRootView` (line 150), or add `accessible={false}` to the backdrop `Animated.View` and keep it on the `Pressable` level only.

---

**7. Toast: `accessibilityLiveRegion="polite"` combined with `accessibilityRole="alert"` sends duplicate announcements on Android**

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/src/components/primitives/Toast.tsx`, lines 183–184

Confidence: 82

On Android, `accessibilityRole="alert"` maps to `android:accessibilityLiveRegion="assertive"` implicitly. Adding `accessibilityLiveRegion="polite"` on the same node causes TalkBack to queue two announcements for the same content — the implicit assertive one fires immediately, and the explicit polite one fires again when the queue drains. For error toasts this means the message is announced twice in rapid succession.

Recommended fix: Remove `accessibilityLiveRegion="polite"` and keep only `accessibilityRole="alert"` (which already implies live-region behavior on both platforms). If "polite" semantics are intentional for non-error variants, gate it:

```tsx
accessibilityLiveRegion={toast.variant === 'error' ? undefined : 'polite'}
accessibilityRole="alert"
```

---

**8. Toast message `Text` node is missing `allowFontScaling={false}`**

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/src/components/primitives/Toast.tsx`, line 187

Confidence: 88

The design spec §1.3 and §8 pre-delivery checklist explicitly state: "Pills, status chips, and tab-bar labels use `allowFontScaling={false}` because their containers are fixed — they're UI chrome, not content." The Codebase Patterns in `progress.txt` also mandates `allowFontScaling={false}` for UI chrome. The toast banner is a fixed-height overlay (UI chrome). At large Dynamic Type sizes the `numberOfLines={3}` clamp will truncate messages at unexpectedly short lengths, and the progress bar / dismiss button will overflow the banner. `allowFontScaling={false}` should be set on the message `Text` (line 187).

Chip.tsx line 98 (`textStyles.micro`) correctly sets `allowFontScaling={false}`. The toast message uses `textStyles.body` without this opt-out.

Recommended fix:
```tsx
<Text
  style={[textStyles.body, styles.message, { color: colors.ink }]}
  numberOfLines={3}
  allowFontScaling={false}
>
```

---

**9. BottomSheet: `dismissFromJS` is a redundant `useCallback` wrapper with no benefit**

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/src/components/primitives/BottomSheet.tsx`, lines 91–93

Confidence: 80

`dismissFromJS` wraps `onDismiss` in a `useCallback` whose only body is `onDismiss()`. The `useMemo` for `pan` closes over `dismissFromJS`, making `dismissFromJS` a dep. But if `onDismiss` changes identity (which it will every render unless the parent memoizes it), `dismissFromJS` also changes, `pan` recreates, and `GestureDetector` replaces the gesture mid-render — potentially losing an in-progress gesture on Android. The indirection adds no value. `runOnJS` can receive `onDismiss` directly, and the `pan` gesture already re-creates whenever `onDismiss` changes via `dismissFromJS` in the dep array. This is not a correctness bug on iOS but is a source of unnecessary gesture re-registration on every parent render that produces a new `onDismiss` reference.

Recommended fix: Pass `onDismiss` directly to `runOnJS` and add it to the `pan` useMemo deps:
```ts
const pan = useMemo(
  () => Gesture.Pan()
    ...
    .onEnd((e) => {
      ...
      if (shouldDismiss) {
        runOnJS(onDismiss)();
        return;
      }
      ...
    }),
  [gestureStart, translateY, snapOffsets, sheetHeight, reduced, onDismiss],
);
```

---

**10. `_layout.tsx`: `StatusBar` rendered outside `ToastProvider` — toast z-index may be clobbered on Android**

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/_layout.tsx`, line 34

Confidence: 80

`<StatusBar style="auto" />` is a sibling of `<ToastProvider>` inside `GestureHandlerRootView`. The toast surface uses `zIndex: 9999` and `elevation: 9999` on its `SafeAreaView` root. On Android, `elevation` creates a stacking context, and a sibling with no elevation rendered after `ToastProvider` in tree order (expo's `StatusBar` renders as a portal-like element) should be fine. However, the `StatusBar` being outside `ToastProvider` means it is not inside the subtree where `ToastSurface` is rendered — this is harmless for the status bar itself. The actual risk is that `ToastSurface` uses `position: 'absolute'` with `top: 0` inside a `SafeAreaView` that itself is a child of the `ToastContext.Provider` — it will position relative to the `ToastProvider`'s containing block, which is `GestureHandlerRootView` (`flex: 1`). This is structurally correct. The issue is that `StatusBar` is placed as the last child inside `GestureHandlerRootView`, which means on some Android versions it could receive a higher stacking order than the toast. Move `StatusBar` inside `ToastProvider` (before `<Slot />`) to keep it unambiguously below the toast layer.

---

## Convention Drift

**11. BottomSheet: `useMemo` wrapping `Gesture.Pan()` diverges from all other primitives; no analog in Button/Chip/SkeletonCard**

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/src/components/primitives/BottomSheet.tsx`, lines 95–129

Confidence: 80

The existing primitives declare `useCallback` for event handlers and `useAnimatedStyle` for animation, but they never wrap gesture constructors in `useMemo`. `Gesture.Pan()` is idiomatic outside `useMemo` with Reanimated gesture handler — the library's own docs and the RNGH source rebuild gestures whenever refs change regardless of memoization. Wrapping in `useMemo` creates a large dep array (6 items) that will frequently invalidate, and the `GestureDetector` must reconcile the new gesture object on every invalidation. This is not wrong but is inconsistent with the rest of the codebase. More importantly, the `reduced` dep is in the array: when the OS reduced-motion setting changes at runtime, the gesture object rebuilds — but this also causes `GestureDetector` to re-attach the recognizer, which cancels any in-progress drag. No other primitive caches reduced-motion-sensitive logic in a `useMemo`.

This is a medium-priority convention issue. The fix is to move the `pan` gesture definition outside of `useMemo` (or keep it but document why memoization is necessary here specifically), matching the simpler, dep-array-free style of the rest of the codebase.

---

## Summary of Issues by Severity

| # | File | Severity | Short description |
|---|---|---|---|
| 5 | Toast.tsx:137 | Critical | `cancelAnimation` in cleanup cancels the freshly-started enter spring on toast replacement |
| 1 | BottomSheet.tsx:74 | Critical | `translateY` initial value goes stale after `sheetHeight` recalculates while sheet is closed |
| 2 | BottomSheet.tsx:161 | Critical | `shadows.hard` overrides the intended upward `shadowOffset` from `styles.sheet`; one is silently discarded |
| 3 | BottomSheet.tsx:150 | Important | Backdrop `Animated.View` missing `pointerEvents="box-none"` — Android gesture routing conflict |
| 4 | Toast.tsx:77 | Important | `clearTimer` not wrapped in `useCallback` — fragile cleanup in unmount effect |
| 6 | BottomSheet.tsx:151 | Important | `accessibilityViewIsModal` on inner view, not root — backdrop Pressable may steal VoiceOver focus |
| 7 | Toast.tsx:183 | Important | `accessibilityLiveRegion="polite"` + `accessibilityRole="alert"` double-announces on Android TalkBack |
| 8 | Toast.tsx:187 | Important | Message `Text` missing `allowFontScaling={false}` — spec §1.3/§8 and Codebase Patterns require it for UI chrome |
| 9 | BottomSheet.tsx:91 | Important | `dismissFromJS` wrapper causes unnecessary pan gesture re-creation on every parent render |
| 10 | _layout.tsx:34 | Important | `StatusBar` rendered after `ToastProvider` in tree — potential Android elevation stacking issue |
| 11 | BottomSheet.tsx:95 | Medium | `useMemo` around `Gesture.Pan()` is inconsistent with all other primitives and has fragile dep array |
